{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4535","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4535/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4535/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4535/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4535","id":2426974875,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6QqLKb","number":4535,"title":"Offload do concurrent to AMD GPUs","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-07-24T08:55:08Z","updated_at":"2024-07-25T01:27:04Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We have to figure out the best approach.

One approach is using our (to be implemented) MLIR backend (#4319) and possibly using an OpenMP MLIR dialect that AMD compilers might be able to offload.

Other ideas are generating C code via our C backend and using AMD C compilers.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4535/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4535/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2249186392","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4535#issuecomment-2249186392","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4535","id":2249186392,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6GD9xY","user":{"login":"sumseq","id":4073260,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNzMyNjA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4073260?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq","html_url":"https://github.com/sumseq","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-07-25T01:27:02Z","updated_at":"2024-07-25T01:27:02Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"It seems NVIDIA uses their OpenACC backend for DC, while Intel uses their OpenMP Target backend.  HPE's CCE also seems to use their OpenACC backend.
I am not sure what the best way to go is.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2249186392/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

working"},{"id":4378061998,"node_id":"LA_kwDOCpMuHc8AAAABBPPsrg","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/labels/parser","name":"parser","color":"75B607","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2023-11-23T03:05:05Z","updated_at":"2023-12-03T21:36:40Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I am hitting this compiler error:

```
syntax error: Token 'data' is unexpected here
   --> hipft.f90:564:7
    |
564 |       data (flow_attenuate_values(i),i=1,MAX_REALIZATIONS) /MAX_REALIZATIONS*-1./
    |       ^^^^ 
```

This data declaration is in a sequence of declaaations insdie a module.

The code compiles and works on GFORTRAN, NVFORTRAN, and IFX
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2899/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2899/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1837607537","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2899#issuecomment-1837607537","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2899","id":1837607537,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc5th6px","user":{"login":"sumseq","id":4073260,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNzMyNjA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4073260?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq","html_url":"https://github.com/sumseq","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sumseq/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-12-03T21:36:39Z","updated_at":"2023-12-03T21:36:39Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Sure!

Here is a reproducer:

```
module number_types
      use iso_fortran_env
      integer, parameter :: r_typ = REAL64
end module

module test
  use number_types
  logical    :: flow_attenuate = .false.
  real(r_typ) :: flow_attenuate_value = 500.0_r_typ
  real(r_typ), dimension(MAX_REALIZATIONS) :: flow_attenuate_values
  data (flow_attenuate_values(i),i=1,MAX_REALIZATIONS) /MAX_REALIZATIONS*-1./
end module

program lfortran_bug_data
use test
implicit none
print*, 'Worked?'
end program
```
